body:
### Problem

mksession adds for each file passed as an argument (nvim A B C) an entry such as  "$argadd <filename>" to Session.vim.

If editing / viewing files is completed and some of the files are closed (:bd) and the session is updated (:mks!), the "$argadd" entry for the closed files are still in the session file.

When closing NeoVim an reopening it later with the session (nvim -S Session.vim) I want to resume my work where I have left of, which means that I only want the files to be loaded that have been loaded when the session has been updated.

But all of the files that have been passed as arguments will be loaded, because of the entries "$argadd <filename>" in Session.vim.

This is very undesired, because "strictly spoken", the in last session I only have had a subset of files loaded.

**Usecase**
As a sysadmin I often open dozens of files to do some careful editing and reviewing:
grep -rl "PATH" git/salt/* | xargs vim

When ever a file review or editing is completed I close it from NeoVim, leaving only the files loaded which still have to be edited / reviewed.

This work may take many hours and may require multiple editing sessions. Before pausing the editing session, I save the session and then close NeoVim. (Actually I am using the plugin persistent.nvim)  

When I have time to resume the work, I want all the files to be loaded **that have not yet been edited / reviewed** and not any of the files that I already have processed / reviewed and closed in previous sessions.

### Steps to reproduce

1. nvim --clean A B C
2. Save session: ':mks!'
3. For each open buffers the session manager adds a line NeoVim has a built in function for session management. This has an   has an undesired behavior, which occurs after the following steps:
1. I open three files in NeoVim such as: 'nvim A B C.
2. Then save the current session with the command: ':mks!'
3. For each open buffer (file) the session manager adds a line 'badd <order-number> <filename>' to the session file Session.vim.:
```
badd +3 ~/.config/A.txt
badd +1 ~/.config/B.txt
badd +1 ~/.config/C.txt
```
4. For each file, provided as an argument when NeoVim has been invoked, it adds a line '$argadd <filename>' to the session file Session.vim:
```
$argadd ~/.config/A.txt
$argadd ~/.config/B.txt
$argadd ~/.config/C.txt

```
5. Closing the file B because it needs no further editing, with the command ':bd'
6. Updating the session file with the command: ':mks!'
7. The line 'badd +1 ~/.config/B.txt' is removed from Session.vim, the line '$argadd ~/.config/B.txt' is not deleted from Session.vim
8. Quitting NeoVim: ':qa'
10. Starting NeoVim: nvim -S Session.vim
11. Instead of only loading the files which have been loaded, when the session has been saved, A and C, also the file B will be loaded, because of the entry: '$argadd ~/.config/B.txt'. This is undesired, because I want to continue my work where I left of last time and I have already completed editing file B and closed it.

Even worse, if I delete file B, because I do not need it anymore, NeoVim will open an empty file B when loading the session. 

I have either to manually remove the entry from Session.vim or by command ":argdel B". 

### Expected behavior

Only the files that have been loaded when the session has been updated must be loaded when NeoVim is started with saved session.

Alternatively NeoVim should support to exclude 'args' from the sessionoptions, which is not the case. This problem even exists if sessionoptions is set to buffers only.

### Nvim version (nvim -v)

-v0.12.1

### Vim (not Nvim) behaves the same?

The VIM documentation states that args can be excluded: ':set sessionoptions-=args'

### Operating system/version

archlinux 6.19.11-arch1-1

### Terminal name/version

kitty 0.46.2

### $TERM environment variable

xterm-kitty

### Installation

Arch Repo

comment_data.body:
> The VIM documentation states that args can be excluded: ':set sessionoptions-=args'

huh? https://github.com/vim/vim/blob/efedcd1e6f369e1dcc92ae0cc2c4c8020f08738e/runtime/doc/options.txt#L7638

> I have either to manually remove the entry from Session.vim or by command ":argdel B".

You can delete all args via : 

    :%argdel

why isn't that acceptable?

labels.description: save/restore editor state: shada, context, ctx, sessions, mksession, undo, swap
labels.name: editor-state

title: mksession ALWAYS adds $argadd which is undesired

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
